definition: The Fed cannot simultaneously maintain data-dependent credibility, price an unobservable structural shift from AI, and avoid policy error. Laubach-Williams r* at ~1.0-1.2% with AI-contingent range of 0.5-2.0%. SEP longer-run dot dispersion widened from ~50bp (2017-2019) to ~125bp+ (2025-2026). SEP longer-run GDP growth unchanged at 1.8-2.0% despite the largest AI investment boom in history. 2020-era institutional transparency makes Greenspan-style model overrides impractical.
evidence: Laubach-Williams r* at ~1.0-1.2% with AI-contingent range of 0.5-2.0%
SEP longer-run dot dispersion widened from ~50bp to ~125bp+
SEP longer-run GDP growth unchanged at 1.8-2.0%
2020-2021 zero-rate hold cited as counterexample but was pandemic (different type of uncertainty)
open_questions: Will the 2025-2026 framework review address AI productivity uncertainty explicitly?
Is the widening SEP dispersion a signal of genuine disagreement or strategic ambiguity?
How does the AI Solow paradox window (2-4 years of maximum policy error risk) resolve?
